title: Linearization Computation
description: Algorithm to compute linear approximation of f(x,y) at point (a,b)
code:
ALGORITHM Linearize(f, a, b)
INPUT: function f(x,y), point (a,b)
OUTPUT: linear approximation L(x,y)

BEGIN
 // Compute function value at expansion point
 f_val ← f(a, b)
 
 // Compute partial derivatives at (a,b)
 fx_val ← (f(a+h, b) - f(a-h, b)) / (2*h) // Numerical partial w.r.t x
 fy_val ← (f(a, b+h) - f(a, b-h)) / (2*h) // Numerical partial w.r.t y
 
 // Return linear approximation function
 L(x, y) ← f_val + fx_val*(x-a) + fy_val*(y-b)
 
 RETURN L
END
explanation: Uses numerical differentiation (central difference) to approximate partial derivatives, then constructs linear approximation L(x,y) = f(a,b) + fx(a,b)(x-a) + fy(a,b)(y-b)

complexity.time: O(1) for each evaluation after derivatives

title: Hessian Matrix Computation
description: Compute Hessian matrix for f(x₁,x₂,...,xₙ) at point a
code:
ALGORITHM ComputeHessian(f, a[], n)
INPUT: function f(x[]), point a[], dimension n
OUTPUT: Hessian matrix H[n][n]

BEGIN
 h ← 0.0001 // Small step for numerical differentiation
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 FOR j FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 // Compute second partial derivative using central difference
 // ∂²f/∂xᵢ∂xⱼ ≈ (f(a+eᵢ+h, a+eⱼ+h) - f(a+eᵢ+h, a+eⱼ-h) 
 // - f(a+eᵢ-h, a+eⱼ+h) + f(a-eᵢ-h, a-eⱼ-h)) / (4*h²)
 
 H[i][j] ← SecondPartial(f, a, i, j, h)
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN H
END

FUNCTION SecondPartial(f, a, i, j, h)
BEGIN
 // Create perturbation vectors
 e_i ← zeros(n); e_i[i] ← 1
 e_j ← zeros(n); e_j[j] ← 1
 
 // Central second difference
 val ← (f(a + h*e_i + h*e_j) - f(a + h*e_i - h*e_j) 
 - f(a - h*e_i + h*e_j) + f(a - h*e_i - h*e_j)) / (4*h*h)
 RETURN val
END
explanation: Computes all second-order partial derivatives using finite differences to build the Hessian matrix, which captures local curvature information

title: Newton-Raphson Method for Systems
description: Solve F(x) = 0 using multivariate Newton's method with Taylor expansion
code:
ALGORITHM NewtonRaphsonSystem(F, J, x0, max_iter, tolerance)
INPUT: Vector function F(x), Jacobian J(x), initial guess x0
OUTPUT: Solution vector x such that F(x) ≈ 0

BEGIN
 x ← x0
 iter ← 0
 
 WHILE iter < max_iter DO
 // Compute F(x) and Jacobian J(x)
 F_val ← F(x)
 J_val ← J(x)
 
 // Check convergence: ||F(x)|| < tolerance
 IF norm(F_val) < tolerance THEN
 RETURN x
 END IF
 
 // Solve J(x) * delta = -F(x) for delta
 delta ← SolveLinearSystem(J_val, -F_val)
 
 // Update: x_new = x_old + delta
 x ← x + delta
 iter ← iter + 1
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN x // May not have converged
END
explanation: Uses first-order Taylor linearization: F(x+δ) ≈ F(x) + J(x)δ = 0, solving Jδ = -F for update step. Quadratic convergence near root if J is invertible.

complexity.time: O(k × n³) where k iterations, n variables, solving linear system O(n³)

title: Quadratic Approximation for Optimization
description: Build quadratic model of objective function for trust-region methods
explanation: Quadratic approximation m(pk) = f(xk) + ∇fᵀpk + ½pkᵀHpk is fundamental in trust-region methods, approximating the objective locally as a paraboloid
